verdict_reason: Reverse-translating the engineering layer back to the verse's Sanskrit structure: (1) yā niśā sarva-bhūtānāṁ tasyāṁ jāgarti saṁyamī → 'the senior is awake to the trade-off being decided, silent through the status performance happening on its surface' (the saṁyamī awake to what is night for ordinary beings); (2) yasyāṁ jāgrati bhūtāni sā niśā paśyato muneḥ → 'the room reverses this: awake to the posturing, blind to the trade-off' (the surface field is night for the muni). The compatibility is verbatim structural. Shankara's specific reading (paramārtha-sat vs vyāvahārika) is operationally rendered as substrate-vs-surface (technical truth vs political weather), which is a faithful operational mapping not a doctrinal reduction. The Vishishtadvaita reading (Self-as-locus-of-devotion vs sense-objects) is also compatible — the senior's loaded-with-technical-truth aperture is a structural analog of the redirected-perceptual-aperture both traditions describe. The falsifiability clause closes the misread that any silent engineer is the muni. No distortion. Pass on both 3.6 and 7.10.
